column1: What is CORS responsible for in API Gateway?
column2: Allowing cross-site access to your APIs; without it, you cannot call the API Gateway service from a browser

column1: What are the three main benefits of serverless stacks?
column2: No server management, flexible scaling, and automated high availability

column1: What type of endpoints does API Gateway support?
column2: Only HTTPS endpoints (does not support HTTP, FTP, or SSH)

column1: What types of origins does CloudFront support for serverless stacks?
column2: A variety of origins including Amazon S3 (supports both static and dynamic data)

column1: Can you run a fully dynamic website in a serverless fashion?
column2: Yes, using JavaScript frameworks like Angular and React with services like S3, API Gateway, Lambda, and DynamoDB

column1: What types of data are good candidates for caching?
column2: Data accessed repeatedly by multiple users (not frequently changing critical data like bank balances)

column1: What caching engines does Amazon ElastiCache support?
column2: Redis and Memcached (MySQL and Couchbase are not caching engines)

column1: What is the default limit for nodes per ElastiCache cluster?
column2: 60 nodes per cluster

column1: What is ElastiCache?
column2: A managed in-memory caching service (not for managing EC2 instances or Elasticsearch)

column1: What do resolvers do in AWS AppSync GraphQL?
column2: Map GraphQL operations (queries and mutations) to data sources like DynamoDB

column1: How do you configure a custom domain for an S3-hosted static website?
column2: Use Amazon Route 53 to manage DNS and direct the custom domain to the S3 bucket

column1: What is the main difference between API Gateway REST APIs and HTTP APIs regarding authorization?
column2: HTTP APIs support out-of-the-box JWT authorization integration; REST APIs have more advanced features but not native JWT support

column1: What API Gateway feature reduces backend processing time and improves performance?
column2: Enabling caching on an API Gateway stage to store and replay frequently accessed responses

column1: What GraphQL feature enables pushing data updates to clients in real time?
column2: Subscriptions (clients don't need to initiate regular queries)

column1: When should you choose Redis over Memcached in ElastiCache?
column2: When you need complex data types (lists, sets, hashes), data persistence, or the ability to sort and rank data

column1: How can you restrict API Gateway access based on IP addresses?
column2: Use an API Gateway resource policy with an IpAddress condition

column1: Does API Gateway support WebSocket APIs?
column2: Yes, WebSocket is a third type of API supported by API Gateway (in addition to HTTP and REST APIs)

column1: Are HTTP APIs generally more or less expensive than REST APIs in API Gateway?
column2: HTTP APIs are generally less expensive than REST APIs

column1: Does increasing Lambda memory improve API performance as effectively as caching?
column2: No, caching is more effective as it reduces the number of times Lambda must be invoked

column1: What is the purpose of API Gateway stages?
column2: To deploy your API and make it live (a resource defines the API but isn't deployed until assigned to a stage)

column1: Can Redis scale horizontally by adding nodes like Memcached?
column2: No, Redis uses replication for scaling reads but is not designed for automatic data partitioning across multiple nodes

column1: Is Memcached or Redis simpler to deploy?
column2: Memcached is simpler; Redis is more complex due to additional features

column1: Does Amazon Cognito handle IP-based filtering?
column2: No, Cognito handles user authentication, not IP-based filtering

column1: Are VPC endpoints suitable for filtering traffic from specific external IPs?
column2: No, VPC endpoints are not suitable for filtering external IP traffic

column1: Can AWS WAF filter IPs for API Gateway?
column2: Yes, but API Gateway resource policies are a simpler, more direct method for IP-based access control
